rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute aspiration pneumonia with objective hypoxaemia (SpO2 89% on room air), tachypnoea with accessory-muscle use, high fever, tachycardia and hypotension (95/60) in a frail, bedbound, non-verbal patient with an unsafe swallow. This is an emerging sepsis / respiratory-failure picture that cannot be delayed and cannot be managed by a telehealth service — immediate 000 ambulance / emergency transfer (T5) is the only appropriate disposition. The definitive treatment (oxygen, parenteral anaerobe-covering antibiotics, sepsis care and monitoring) is hospital-level. Escalation should be consistent with any documented advance care directive / goals of care; absent a documented ceiling, treat as a full emergency.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening breathing, rising respiratory rate, oxygen saturations staying below 90% or falling despite oxygen, or lips/fingertips turning blue
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately and keep them on the line; do not wait
escalation_edges.rationale: Progressive hypoxaemic respiratory failure is imminent and needs paramedic airway/oxygen support without delay.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If his breathing gets worse, his oxygen stays below 90% or drops despite oxygen, or his lips or fingertips go blue — call 000 straight away and tell them it is a breathing emergency.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Blood pressure dropping further (systolic below 90), becoming drowsy, unresponsive or unarousable
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals septic shock / decompensation requiring immediate resuscitation and transfer.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If his blood pressure drops further, or he becomes drowsy, hard to rouse or unresponsive, call 000 immediately — this is septic shock until proven otherwise.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: He stops breathing normally or becomes unresponsive with no normal breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 and begin CPR now if trained; use the facility resuscitation plan
escalation_edges.rationale: Respiratory/cardiac arrest — dispatcher-guided CPR and paramedic resuscitation are the critical resources, subject to the patient's documented resuscitation status.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he stops breathing normally or can't be roused, call 000 now and start CPR if you are trained and it is consistent with his resuscitation plan.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Advanced neurodegenerative disease with bulbar palsy, unsafe swallow and recurrent aspiration
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: High risk of rapid deterioration and further aspiration; strengthens the case for immediate escalation and strict nil-by-mouth, and mandates a swallow/diet review after stabilisation.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Non-verbal, totally dependent resident; all information is second-hand from facility nursing staff
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for escalation because the patient cannot self-report deterioration; direct staff to stay with him and monitor breathing and responsiveness continuously until paramedics arrive.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Frail older adult with recent weight loss / undernutrition
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Higher risk of severe sepsis and poor physiological reserve; do not be reassured by a 'stable' baseline.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Nursing-home resident with advanced disease — possible documented goals-of-care / ceiling of treatment
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Check for and honour any advance care directive; if a ceiling of care is documented, align the response (which may be comfort-focused) with it. Absent a documented ceiling, escalate as a full emergency.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an emergency ambulance now and say it is a breathing emergency and suspected chest infection with low oxygen. While you wait: sit him upright, apply any available oxygen aiming for above 92%, keep him strictly nil by mouth (no food, fluids or oral medicines), have suction ready and use it if you are trained, and stay with him watching his breathing and responsiveness. Have his medication chart and any advance care plan ready for the paramedics.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for a daytime medical round.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Send a transfer letter, current medication chart, recent weight-loss and swallowing history, observations, and any advance care directive / goals-of-care documentation with the patient. A speech-pathology swallow review and aspiration-prevention care plan should be arranged after the acute episode.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating a hypoxaemic, hypotensive, febrile frail patient as a routine or same-day problem, or dismissing it as baseline neurodegenerative disease.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Progression to hypoxaemic respiratory failure and septic shock while managed in place; preventable death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising ED by self/facility transport without emphasising the time-critical 000 ambulance need in a patient already at SpO2 89% with hypotension.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Not applicable — T5 is the correct tier for this presentation.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot auscultate, image the chest or measure gases, so the exact chest diagnosis (aspiration pneumonia vs another pneumonia, and co-existing PE or heart failure) cannot be confirmed remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the severity signals (hypoxaemia, tachypnoea, hypotension) and escalate immediately — the disposition does not depend on pinning the exact diagnosis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Delaying escalation to gather more information, or downgrading urgency because the diagnosis is not radiologically confirmed.
